easiest-cranberry-sauce.name: Easiest Cranberry Sauce
instructions:
Cook 1 (12-oz.) bag fresh or frozen cranberries, ¾ cup granulated sugar, 1–2 Tbsp. Grand Marnier, Cointreau, or other orange liqueur (if using), 1 Tbsp. finely grated orange zest, a pinch of kosher salt, and 1 cup water in a medium saucepan over medium heat, stirring often to keep sauce from sticking to bottom of pan and burning, until cranberries start to burst and become jammy (sauce should coat a spoon), about 10 minutes. Let cool before serving.
Do ahead: Cranberry sauce can be made 5 days ahead. Transfer to an airtight container; cover and refrigerate until well chilled.
Editor’s note: This easy cranberry sauce recipe was first printed on our website in an article titled “Cranberry Sauce Is the Last Thing You Should Stress About on Thanksgiving.” Head this way for more of our best Thanksgiving recipes →

easter-eggs.name: Easter Eggs
instructions: Beat together cream cheese, margarine, and vanilla in a large bowl with an electric mixer until well combined. Add confectioners' sugar in batches, beating well after each addition. Dough will be very stiff.
Divide dough equally into 2 bowls. Stir peanut butter into one bowl and coconut into the other until incorporated.
Using your hands, mold dough into egg shapes and arrange them on cookie sheets. Place molded eggs in the freezer until frozen, 1 to 2 hours.
Melt together chocolate chips and shortening on top of a double boiler until smooth. Allow to cool slightly at room temperature.
Dip frozen eggs into melted chocolate mixture until completely coated. Place dipped eggs on wax paper-lined cookie sheets and return to the freezer to harden. Store eggs in the refrigerator.

easter-mona-traditional-recipe-pequerece.name: Easter Mona (traditional Recipe)

ingredients.item: 20 g de levadura fresca (o 7 g de levadura seca de panadero)
80 ml de aceite de oliva suave
Opcional: 1 chorrito de agua de azahar
1 yema de huevo y 1 cucharada de leche para pintar
Eggs (quantity according to the monkeys we make)
50 g of smoked sugar with a few drops of water

instructions: We put 50 of force flour into a bowl, we put a hole in the centre and we put 50 ml of temperate milk into the hole. We added the fresh beamed leaven into the milk and mix them all well with a fork till we have some kind of paddle. We cover with a moist cloth and let it rest about 20 or 30 minutes, till the primer starts bubbling.

While resting the primer, we put to heat 100 million milk by adding a cinnamon branch and half-orange skin previously washed. Before she starts cooking, we remove her from the fire and let her cool till she's warm.

In a big bowl we mix the other ingredients: 450 g of force flour, 120 g of sugar, 2 eggs, 80 ml of soft olive oil, a pint of azahar water and a pint of salt. We put up already temperate flavoured milk and add it to the dough and mix them up. Finally we add the preference that we have let rest and we re-mix everything onto a slightly pitted surface we vertir the mass and amasamos for about 10 or 15 minutes until the mass remains elastic and bright. At first, it's normal for the dough to be very sticky, and if we get high we can put some oil or flour into our hands so we can work better. Alternatively, if you have an amasador or kitchen robot you can amaswith them for about 5-10 minutes.
Once we have finished amasing, we form a ball and put it into a bowl covered with a moist cloth and let it be lifted for about an hour and a half, until it has doubled its size.

Once the mass has been lifted up sufficiently, we divide the mass into pieces according to the quantity and size of the monkeys you wish to make. With these amounts, we have made four pieces of equal size, to make individual monkeys but you can also divide them into two to make medium-sized monkeys.
From each of the pieces of mass we form balls by briefly chewing them up and slightly crushing them.
At each of the mass balls, we put a hole at the centre with our fingers and put an egg into the hole, to give them the typical shape of the Valencian Easter monkeys.

In a small bowl we mix an egg with a tablespoon of milk and paint the monkeys with a brush. We cut the oven plate with baking paper and carefully place the Easter monkeys and leave some space between them Let them up a second time in a warm place for about 45-60 minutes. Meanwhile we preheat the oven at 180 ° with heat up and down Once they have grown in size we re-paint the monkeys with an egg and milk mixture, with great care and without squeezing so as to avoid deinflating the mass. We put a few drops of water at 50 g of sugar to moist and distribute it to the surface of the dough at our best.

We finally bake for about 15- 20 minutes if they are individual or 20- 25 if they are medium-sized monkeys If we see that they are getting too dyed up, we can cover them half as soon as we have some aluminum paper. When they're ready, we get them out of the oven and we let them get cold in a grid. If we want to we can remove the eggs with which we have baked them and trade them with colorful dyed eggs, and we have our Easter Fines ready for snack.

easter-veal-recipe-teleculinaria.name: Easter Veal Recipe
instructions: Cut the veal rib into large pieces and throw them into a bowl. Season with salt, pepper, chopped garlic, leaves of laurel, colorau, white wine, vinegar and olive oil, mix well and let marinate in the cold overnight.

In the day, lay the veal on a tray, add the marinade and bring it to the oven preheated at 180oC for 1 hour and 45 minutes, turning and watering from time to time. Peel the chips, wash them, add them to the veal as soon as it gets golden, wrap them well in the sauce and let them bake until the potatoes are golden.

In the meantime, wash the mushrooms and cut them in half or in rooms. Cut the bacon into small pieces, lay them down in a frying pan, take it to the fire without adding fat and let them cook until they get golden. Remove and reserve the bacon, add the mushrooms to the same frying pan with the fat that the bacon released and let them cook until golden and liquid-free. Add the bacon again, mix, refine the salt, season with a pinch and pepper and serve to accompany the veal with the chips. Sprinkle everything with chopped parsley.

egg-lettuce-tomato-sandwich.name: Egg Lettuce Tomato Sandwich
instructions: Spread both sides of bread slices all the way to the edges with the thinnest layer of mayonnaise possible.
Heat a dry large skillet over medium-low; cook bread until golden, about 3 minutes per side.
Transfer to a plate; let cool.
Spread remaining mayonnaise over both slices of bread.
Top a slice with tomatoes; season generously with salt and pepper.
Arrange eggs over tomatoes; season.
Top with lettuce, close sandwich, and cut in half.

egg-free-royal-icing.name: Egg-free Royal Icing
instructions: Stir together confectioners' sugar and milk in a small bowl until smooth. Beat in corn syrup and almond extract until royal icing is smooth and glossy. Add food coloring to your desired intensity.

eggplant-risotto-recipe-step-by-step-com.name: Eggplant Risotto Recipe Step By Step
eggplant-risotto-recipe-step-by-step-com.original_name: Receta de risotto de berenjenas paso a paso

ingredients.item: 100 gr queso manchego  ((grana padano o parmesano))

instructions: Comienza la receta pelando y picando finamente la cebolla.
Calienta la mitad de la mantequilla en una cazuela y dora la cebolla a fuego lento unos siete u ocho minutos.
Wash the eggplant and remove the end with the stem, cut it into small dice. Do the same with the tomato.
Add it to the casserole and continue to cook for 5 minutes till you are tender.
Incorpora el arroz y rehoga un par de minutos sin dejar de remover.
Add the wine and sugar, put up the fire and let the alcohol evaporate before adding a large tablespoon of the vegetable stock.
El caldo debe estar caliente debe irse agregando de apoco una vez que sea absorbido por el arroz.
No dejes de remover.
When they have been after 16 minutes of cooking, try the point of the rice to make sure that it's with the dente.
Withdraw from the fire when the grain is tender and slightly hard inside.
At the end you have to add the rest of the butter and the grated cheese.
Remueve para incorporar y espera un par de minutos antes de servir.

eggs-caviar.name: Eggs Caviar

ingredients.item: Grams of your favorite caviar or roe

instructions: Gently whip the eggs with a fork.

Heat a saucepan over medium heat, then add the Cognac and butter (they should sizzle/simmer immediately). Add the eggs and whisk constantly—working on and off the heat in order to develop the curds little by little and prevent the eggs from sticking or otherwise overcooking at the base of the pan—until the eggs begin to resemble a soft scramble. Add 1 tablespoon of the sour cream and stir to incorporate, letting the eggs sputter and pop a few times on the heat. Barely fold in the remaining sour cream (some streaks should still be visible).

Top the buttered toast with plenty of soft scrambled eggs and a heaping spoon of the caviar. Or pour eggs in a bowl and top the whole thing with the caviar, use the toast as a spoon, and knock yourself out.

eggs-over-with-potato-hash.name: Eggs Over With Potato Hash
instructions: {'level': '1', 'description': 'Place bacon in a skillet and cook over medium-high heat, turning occasionally, until evenly browned, about 10 minutes. Drain bacon slices on paper towels.'}

{'level': '1', 'description': 'Add ghee to the bacon drippings. Add potato, onion, 1/2 teaspoon salt, and 1/2 teaspoon pepper. Cook and stir until potatoes are tender, about 10 minutes, adding kale during the last few minutes. Remove potatoes to a plate and crumble bacon slices over top.'}

{'level': '1', 'description': 'When pan has cooled to low, crack eggs into the pan. Season with remaining salt and pepper. Cook until eggs are almost completely set but not hard, 1 1/2 to 2 minutes.'}
{'level': '1', 'description': 'Carefully flip eggs and cook until whites are completely set, about 30 second more.'}
{'level': '1', 'description': 'Gently slide eggs on top of the potato hash. Drizzle with truffle oil and serve.'}

enjoy-the-hawaiian-chops-comedera.name: Enjoy The Hawaiian Chops
enjoy-the-hawaiian-chops-comedera.original_name: Disfruta de las chuletas a la hawaiana

ingredients.item: ½ pineapple can in syrup (with slices)

instructions: To start the recipe we have to seal the chops into a frying pan with some butter till they have a golden color. Reserve.
That same frying pan heats up the rest of the butter and pits the garlic with the onion for a few minutes.
Add pineapple syrup, english salsa, catsup, sugar and sauonamos with some pepper. Cooking for 5 minutes or till thick.
Go back to the frying pan and cover them with pineapple cuts. Add water and cover.
Both pineapple and pineapple chops are best glazed with salsa. Cooking for 3 more minutes and serving at once.

erins-cheesy-broccoli-soup.name: Erin's Cheesy Broccoli Soup
instructions: Bring water and chicken bouillon granules to a boil in a large pot. Add broccoli and onion; cook at a boil until tender, 10 to 12 minutes.

Combine milk, cream cheese, margarine, and flour in a second large pot over medium heat. Cook and stir until margarine melts and mixture begins to thicken, about 5 minutes. Stir in Cheddar cheese until melted completely.
Pour broccoli mixture into cheese mixture; stir well.

espresso-hot-chocolate.name: Espresso Hot Chocolate

ingredients.item: Lightly sweetened whipped cream and chocolate-covered espresso beans

instructions: Bring milk and cinnamon to a simmer in a medium saucepan over medium-low heat, whisking occasionally and making sure milk doesn’t boil, until cinnamon is floral and fragrant, about 10 minutes. Whisk in chocolate, sugar, and salt and cook, whisking frequently, until mixture is smooth and creamy and chocolate is melted, about 5 minutes. Pour through a fine-mesh sieve into a large measuring cup; discard cinnamon.
Add espresso to hot chocolate mixture and stir to combine. Divide among mugs, then top with whipped cream and espresso beans, if desired.

even-better-cheesy-potatoes.name: Even Better Cheesy Potatoes
instructions: Preheat the oven to 177°C (175 degrees C).
Melt 6 tablespoons butter in a skillet over medium heat. Add onion and garlic; cook and stir until onion is slightly softened, 5 to 10 minutes. Set aside off heat.
Combine potatoes, Mexican cheese blend, sour cream, condensed soup, and onion-garlic mixture in a bowl; transfer to a 9x13-inch baking dish.
Combine corn flakes, 2 tablespoons melted butter, and garlic salt in a bowl; sprinkle over potato mixture. Cover the baking dish with aluminum foil.
Bake in the preheated oven for 30 minutes. Remove foil; continue baking until topping is lightly browned, about 30 minutes more.

every-night-salad-with-yogurt-ranch.name: Every Night Salad With Yogurt Ranch

ingredients.item: A small handful of mixed herbs
ingredients.prep: such as dill, basil, cilantro, and/or parsley

instructions: Peel 1 garlic clove and use a Microplane to finely grate into a medium bowl. If we're putting garlic into a dressing, we're almost always going to grate it. It's faster and easier than mincing by hand, and ensures that the garlic flavor is evenly dispersed.

Slice 4 sides off 2 lemons. Cutting the lemons this way makes it easy to remove the seeds, and creates easy-to-squeeze "cheeks." Squeeze juice from 1 lemon into bowl with garlic. Set remaining cheeks aside.

Add ½ cup yogurt, ¼ cup mayonnaise, 2 dashes hot sauce, ½ tsp. salt, and ½ tsp. pepper and whisk to combine. Give the dressing a taste and add more salt and pepper if necessary—it should be plenty punchy.

Remove leaves from any tough stems from handful of herbs. (You can use whatever soft herbs you have lying around for this, alone or in combination, but the dressing will taste great even without them.) Finely chop leaves, then stir into yogurt mixture.

Crush ¼ cup roasted, salted almonds with the bottom of a small pot or pan. You can certainly toast raw almonds yourself if you want some extra credit, but using ready-to-go nuts is a good option if you don't feel like turning on the oven. Don't like almonds? Any nut or seed will work great here, too.
Cut off bottom of 1 head of romaine and let leaves fall from core; discard any leaves that are tough, leathery, or wilty. Continue cutting from the bottom, removing leaves as they fall.

Once you have removed all the leaves, slice larger leaves in half and leave smaller ones whole. (If your leaves are dirty or gritty, this is a good time to give them a wash—just remember to dry them off as thoroughly as possible afterward.) Transfer to a large bowl.

Squeeze reserved lemon cheeks over lettuce and toss gently to coat. (We always dress with acid—think citrus or vinegar—before adding any oil, otherwise it will slide right off.) Add 2 Tbsp. oil and a generous pinch of salt; toss again to coat. If it seems like you're dressing this salad twice, well, you are! This ensures that each leaf of lettuce is properly seasoned before that creamy ranch gets drizzled on—a pro move whenever you're dressing a salad.
Arrange half of lettuce mixture on a platter. Spoon dressing over, then repeat with remaining lettuce and dressing. Top with almonds; season with plenty of pepper. Repeat every night from now on.

everything-bagel-crescent-bites.name: Everything Bagel Crescent Bites

ingredients.item: Garlic and herb cream cheese spread

instructions: Preheat the oven to 191°C (190 degrees C). Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.

Unroll dough on a clean surface. Roll out dough, if necessary, into a 8x12-inch rectangle. Using a pizza cutter or knife, cut the dough into 6 rows by 4 rows to make 24 squares. Divide cream cheese amongst each square, placing approximately 3/4 teaspoon in the middle of each. Bring all 4 corners up over the cheese and pinch the corners together lightly; place on the prepared baking sheet.
Whisk together egg and water and lightly brush over each crescent bite. Sprinkle with bagel seasoning.
Bake in the preheated oven until lightly browned, 10 to 12 minutes. Let cool slightly before eating as filling will be hot.

explore-the-flavor-of-grouper-a-la-menie.name: Explore The Flavor Of Grouper A La Menier, A Culinary Secret From The French Coast
explore-the-flavor-of-grouper-a-la-menie.original_name: Explora el sabor del mero a la menier, un secreto culinario de la costa francesa

ingredients.item: 2 cdas de perejil fresco, finamente picado
4  filetes de mero ((aprox. 200 g cada uno))

instructions: We started the recipe by preparing the menier sauce. In a frying pan with medium heat, melt the butter till I get a golden color. That process Highlights the steamed flavour of butter.
Once the butter's golden, add the finely minced parsley. Cooking for about 30 seconds so that parsley will free its scent.
Remove frying pan from the fire and add fresh lemon juice. Mix well to combine flavors and adjust the season with salt and pepper to suit. And now she's ready.
Ahora vamos a sazonar los filetes de mero con sal y pimienta al gusto.
Pass the steaks for flour, and then for whipped egg and finally for steamed almonds and make sure you cover them completely.
Cooking the mero steaks for 3-4 minutes for each side or until they are golden and cooked inside.
Sirve los filetes de mero con la salsa menier y sirve.
